description: Chess engine with material+minimax evaluation, Stockfish benchmarking, CSV match logging, and evolution tracking reports

userStories.title: Download and configure Stockfish
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to download Stockfish so I can use it as a benchmark engine.
acceptanceCriteria: Shell script scripts/download-stockfish.sh downloads the Stockfish binary for Linux x86_64
Binary is placed at engines/stockfish and made executable
engines/ directory is added to .gitignore
Script is idempotent (skips download if binary already exists)
Running 'echo uci | engines/stockfish' outputs 'uciok'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate chess-runner match series with Stockfish
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to confirm chess-runner runs a multi-game match correctly with real UCI engines.
acceptanceCriteria: Running chess-runner --engine1 engines/stockfish --engine2 engines/stockfish --games 2 --time 5000 completes without errors
Colors alternate correctly between games
Final summary correctly tallies wins and draws
No panics or protocol errors during the match
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 for Stockfish binary. This is a validation story — fix any bugs found in chess-runner or chesslib.

userStories.title: Create chess-engine crate skeleton
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a new chess-engine crate in the workspace as a dedicated place for our custom engine.
acceptanceCriteria: New chess-engine/ directory with Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs, and src/main.rs
chess-engine added to workspace members in root Cargo.toml
Cargo.toml depends on chesslib (path = '../chesslib')
src/lib.rs has a placeholder module structure (pub mod eval; pub mod search; pub mod engine;) with empty modules
src/main.rs has a minimal main() that prints the engine name
cargo build -p chess-engine compiles without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement material evaluation function
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an evaluation function based on piece values so the engine can score positions.
acceptanceCriteria: Function evaluate(board: &Board) -> i32 in chess-engine/src/eval.rs
Returns centipawn score from side-to-move's perspective
Piece values: Pawn=100, Knight=320, Bishop=330, Rook=500, Queen=900
Positive when side-to-move has material advantage, negative when behind
Checkmate detected (no legal moves + in check) returns -30000 (losing side perspective)
Stalemate detected (no legal moves + not in check) returns 0
Test: starting position evaluates to 0
Test: position missing a white queen evaluates negative for white
Test: known checkmate position returns extreme value
Test: known stalemate position returns 0
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 for crate skeleton

userStories.title: Implement negamax search
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a negamax search so the engine can look ahead and choose the best move.
acceptanceCriteria: Function search(board: &Board, depth: u8) -> (ChessMove, i32) in chess-engine/src/search.rs
Uses negamax formulation (recursive, negates child scores)
At depth 0, returns the material evaluation from eval.rs
Returns the best legal move and its score for any non-terminal position
Test: finds mate-in-1 (e.g. queen can deliver checkmate in one move)
Test: avoids hanging a queen when a safe alternative exists
Test: returns a valid legal move from the starting position
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 for evaluation function

userStories.title: Implement Engine trait (in-process MaterialEngine)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MaterialEngine to implement the Engine trait so it can run in-process with the match runner.
acceptanceCriteria: Struct MaterialEngine in chess-engine/src/engine.rs that holds a configurable depth (default 4)
Implements chesslib::engine::Engine trait (async_trait)
name() returns 'MaterialEngine v0.1'
go() calls the negamax search with the configured depth
Integration test: MaterialEngine vs MaterialEngine completes a game via Match::run() without panicking
Integration test: game result is a valid GameResult variant (not Ongoing)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 for search. Add async-trait and tokio as dependencies.

userStories.title: UCI protocol loop in chess-engine binary
userStories.description: As a developer, I want chess-engine to run as a UCI binary so it works with chess-runner and external tools.
acceptanceCriteria: src/main.rs reads lines from stdin and implements UCI protocol
Responds to 'uci' with 'id name MaterialEngine v0.1', 'id author chess-ic', and 'uciok'
Responds to 'isready' with 'readyok'
Handles 'position startpos moves e2e4 e7e5 ...' — builds Board by applying moves
Handles 'position fen <fen> moves ...' — builds Board from FEN then applies moves
Handles 'go wtime W btime B winc WI binc BI' — runs search and responds with 'bestmove <uci_move>'
Handles 'ucinewgame' (resets internal state) and 'quit' (exits process)
Accepts --depth <N> CLI flag to set search depth (default 4)
Works as engine in chess-runner: chess-runner --engine1 ./target/release/chess-engine --engine2 engines/stockfish --games 1 --time 60000
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-006. Use clap for CLI flags. UCI parsing is synchronous stdin line reading.

userStories.title: CSV match result logging in chess-runner
userStories.description: As a developer, I want chess-runner to log match results to a CSV file for tracking over time.
acceptanceCriteria: New --output <path> flag on chess-runner CLI
After a match completes, appends one row to the CSV with: timestamp (ISO 8601), engine1_name, engine2_name, games_played, engine1_wins, engine2_wins, draws, engine1_win_rate (as decimal 0.0-1.0)
Engine names are read from the UCI 'id name' response (not the file path)
Creates the file with header row if it doesn't exist
Appends without overwriting if file already exists
If --output is not specified, no CSV is written (backwards compatible)
Add csv crate as dependency to chess-runner
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 for engine names via UCI. Needs to query engine names before logging.

userStories.title: Evolution report CLI command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a report command that reads the CSV and prints a text summary of engine progress.
acceptanceCriteria: New subcommand: chess-runner report --input <csv_path>
Refactor chess-runner to use clap subcommands: 'match' (existing behavior) and 'report' (new)
Report displays a formatted text table with columns: date, engine1, engine2, games, wins, losses, draws, win_rate
Shows overall summary line: total games played, overall win rate, best matchup, worst matchup
If CSV has multiple entries for the same engine pair, shows trend arrow (up/down/stable)
Handles missing CSV file with message: 'No match history found at <path>'
Handles empty CSV (header only) with message: 'No match results recorded yet'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 for CSV format. Uses the csv crate to read the file.
